initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
nr(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
nc(M) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

row(R) :- nr(N), R >= 0, R < N.
col(C) :- nc(M), C >= 0, C < M.

% Nonzero cells
bind(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column
countcol(C,N) :- N = #count{R : bind(R,C,V)}.

% Number of output cells in a column
selCount(C,K) :- K = #count{R : output(R,C,Color)}.

% Number of nonzero cells above a cell in the same column
cntAbove(R,C,N) :- bind(R,C,V), N = #count{R2 : bind(R2,C,V), R2 < R}.

% Constraints
:- selCount(C,K), K <= 2.
:- selCount(C,K), col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 0, K >= 1.
:- countcol(C,N), N > 1, selCount(C,K), K = 2.
:- cntAbove(R,C,N), output(R,C,Color), N <= 1.

% Output predicate
output(R,C,V) :- bind(R,C,V).

% Choose cells:
%  If a column has at least two nonzero cells, pick exactly two (the two lowest rows).
%  If a column has only one nonzero cell, pick that single cell.
2 { output(R,C,Color) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 2 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 1.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 1 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N = 1.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions
nr(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
nc(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

row(R) :- nr(N), R >= 0, R < N.
col(C) :- nc(M), C >= 0, C < M.

% Nonzero cells
bind(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column
countcol(C,N) :- bind(R1,C,V1), N = #count{R2 : bind(R2,C,V2), R2 < R1}.

% Number of output cells in a column
selCount(C,K) :- col(C), bind(R1,C,V1), K = #count{R2 : output(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0}.

% Number of nonzero cells above a cell in the same column
cntAbove(R,C,N) :- bind(R,C,V), N = #count{R2 : bind(R2,C,V), R2 < R}.

% Constraints
:- selCount(C,K), K <= 2.
:- selCount(C,K), col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 0, K >= 1.
:- countcol(C,N), N > 1, selCount(C,K), K = 2.
:- cntAbove(R,C,N), output(R,C,Color), N <= 1.

% Output predicate
output(R,C,V) :- bind(R,C,V).

% Choose cells:
%  If a column has at least two nonzero cells, pick exactly two (the two lowest rows).
%  If a column has only one nonzero cell, pick that single cell.
2 { output(R,C,V) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 2 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 1.
1 { output(R,C,V) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 1 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N = 1.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions
nr(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
nc(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

row(R) :- nr(N), R >= 0, R < N.
col(C) :- nc(M), C >= 0, C < M.

% Nonzero cells
bind(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column
countcol(C,N) :- bind(R1,C,V1), N = #count{R2 : bind(R2,C,V2), R2 < R1}.

% Number of output cells in a column
selCount(C,K) :- col(C), bind(R1,C,V1), K = #count{R2 : output(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0}.

% Number of nonzero cells above a cell in the same column
cntAbove(R,C,N) :- bind(R,C,V), N = #count{R2 : bind(R2,C,V), R2 < R}.

% Constraints
:- selCount(C,K), K <= 2.
:- selCount(C,K), col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 0, K >= 1.
:- countcol(C,N), N > 1, selCount(C,K), K = 2.
:- cntAbove(R,C,N), output(R,C,Color), N <= 1.

% Output predicate
output(R,C,V) :- bind(R,C,V).

% Choose cells:
%  If a column has at least two nonzero cells, pick exactly two (the two lowest rows).
%  If a column has only one nonzero cell, pick that single cell.
2 { output(R,C,V) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 2 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 1.
1 { output(R,C,V) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 1 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N = 1.

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Grid dimensions
nr(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
nc(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

row(R) :- nr(N), R >= 0, R < N.
col(C) :- nc(M), C >= 0, C < M.

% Nonzero cells
bind(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column
countcol(C,N) :- bind(R1,C,V1), N = #count{R2 : bind(R2,C,V2), R2 < R1}.

% Number of output cells in a column
selCount(C,K) :- col(C), bind(R1,C,V1), K = #count{R2 : output(R2,C,V2), V2 != 0}.

% Number of nonzero cells above a cell in the same column
cntAbove(R,C,N) :- bind(R,C,V), N = #count{R2 : bind(R2,C,V), R2 < R}.

% Constraints
:- selCount(C,K), K <= 2.
:- selCount(C,K), col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 0, K >= 1.
:- countcol(C,N), N > 1, selCount(C,K), K = 2.
:- cntAbove(R,C,N), output(R,C,Color), N <= 1.

% Output predicate
output(R,C,V) :- bind(R,C,V).

% Choose cells:
%  If a column has at least two nonzero cells, pick exactly two (the two lowest rows).
%  If a column has only one nonzero cell, pick that single cell.
2 { output(R,C,V) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 2 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N > 1.
1 { output(R,C,V) : bind(R,C,V), R > 0 } 1 :- col(C), countcol(C,N), N = 1.

#show output/3.
